description: Build Diagnostic Trio as a modular diagnostic tool family that integrates Discover, Searcher, and Trace around a shared workspace, evidence contract, and journal flow without bloating Diagnostic Quartet core.

userStories.title: Create the Diagnostic Trio project scaffold
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a baseline project structure so Trio can be implemented as a standalone tool.
acceptanceCriteria: Create the initial repository structure for Rust host execution and Python reasoning components
Add top-level project documentation describing the purpose of Diagnostic Trio
Add baseline development configuration needed to run type and test checks for the chosen stack
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Define the shared evidence schema
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a common evidence schema so Discover, Searcher, and Trace emit compatible findings.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a shared evidence record definition with fields for source tool, timestamp, target, probe family, layer, status, summary, raw references or excerpts, confidence, and interpretation
Document allowed status values including blocked, pass, fail, partial, and not-tested
Document how static and runtime evidence are distinguished in the shared schema
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Define the shared workspace and state contract
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a shared workspace contract so Trio tools exchange state and artifacts consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: Define the workspace layout Trio expects for artifacts, caches, journal data, and shared state
Document the shared state inputs required by Discover, Searcher, and Trace
Document how Trio reads from and writes back to its workspace without depending on Quartet internals
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement the layer and status model
userStories.description: As an engineer, I want a reusable layer model so findings are classified consistently across Trio capabilities.
acceptanceCriteria: Define Layers 1 through 7: Physical, Data Link, Network, Transport, Session, Presentation, and Application
Define rules preventing upper layers from being treated as healthy when lower layers are failed or untested
Expose the layer and diagnostic status model for reuse by Discover, Searcher, and Trace
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add Discover evidence classification
userStories.description: As an engineer, I want Discover to classify findings by layer so likely failure surfaces are easier to isolate.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement Discover classification for repository and host evidence into layer-tagged findings
Discover records blocked, pass, fail, partial, or not-tested status on each finding
Discover outputs use the shared evidence schema
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add Searcher backend catalog
userStories.description: As an engineer, I want Searcher to know its supported backends so search capability selection and fallback behavior are consistent.
acceptanceCriteria: Define backend categories covering high-performance, specialized, Windows-compatible, optional interactive, and related text-processing search tools
Document which search backends are preferred and which are optional fallbacks
Represent backend capabilities in a normalized form that other Trio components can consume
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement Searcher result normalization
userStories.description: As an engineer, I want Searcher outputs normalized by intent so results from different tools can be interpreted consistently.
acceptanceCriteria: Support search intent categories for config lookup, error-string hunt, entry-point tracing, dependency tracing, schema or payload search, and secret or config surface detection
Normalize file, config, log, source-code, structured-text, and generated-artifact results into the shared evidence schema
Map search results into layer-aware meaning where applicable
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Implement Trace runtime evidence capture
userStories.description: As an engineer, I want Trace to collect live runtime evidence so behavior unavailable from static inspection can still be diagnosed.
acceptanceCriteria: Support runtime interrogation targets including processes, listeners and open ports, routes and interfaces, sessions, logs, payload or protocol behavior, runtime dependency behavior, and bind or transport anomalies
Distinguish dynamic runtime evidence from static repository evidence in Trace outputs
Emit Trace findings using the shared evidence schema
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add runtime safety gating
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want runtime inspection to be safe by default so Trio remains read-only unless explicitly authorized otherwise.
acceptanceCriteria: Default Discover and Trace behavior is non-destructive and read-only
Potentially mutating or sensitive inspection paths are explicitly gated
Safety and provenance expectations are documented for runtime inspection workflows
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Integrate a shared journal
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want important Trio work appended to a shared journal so the audit trail remains continuous.
acceptanceCriteria: Create a journal workflow for Trio that captures materially important work from Discover, Searcher, and Trace
Document the journal format and append-only behavior
Link journal entries to evidence records or findings where applicable
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Define artifact write-back behavior
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want Trio findings written into durable artifacts so investigations remain reviewable after a run ends.
acceptanceCriteria: Define the artifact set Trio maintains or updates from Discover, Searcher, and Trace findings
Ensure write-back behavior preserves provenance to the originating evidence records
Document how the artifact update flow relates to the shared journal and workspace
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Separate Rust execution from Python reasoning
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want clear language boundaries so host execution remains portable and reasoning remains isolated.
acceptanceCriteria: Define Rust responsibilities for CLI behavior, host interaction, orchestration, normalization, and MCP exposure
Define Python responsibilities for reasoning, interpretation, synthesis, and narrative generation
Document the interface between Rust and Python components
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Add search backend fallback behavior
userStories.description: As an operator, I want Trio to degrade gracefully when preferred search tools are unavailable on the host.
acceptanceCriteria: Searcher prefers fast local backends when available
Searcher falls back cleanly when preferred tools are missing
Fallback behavior preserves normalized output shape and provenance
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Document family architecture and operating flows
userStories.description: As a future contributor, I want the Trio family structure documented so the system can evolve without collapsing into a monolith.
acceptanceCriteria: Document initial setup, layered diagnosis, and maintenance or drift flows for Trio
Document the relationship between Discover, Searcher, and Trace as companion capabilities
Document extension points for new probe families or MCP exposure without changing the core evidence model
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
